trivia_bullets.text: Gatecrash (released February 1, 2013) is the second set in the Return to Ravnica block, focusing on the five guilds not featured in Return to Ravnica: Orzhov, Dimir, Gruul, Boros, and Simic. Kingpin's Pet is a Common in the Orzhov guild slot (watermark: Orzhov). [Scryfall: Kingpin's Pet, released_at 2013-02-01, set_name Gatecrash, watermark orzhov]

Flavor text: "Debt and death sound so similar, and thrulls are so hard of hearing. Mistakes are inevitable." — Milana, Orzhov prelate [Scryfall]. The quote encodes the Orzhov's core extractive mechanics: 'debt' and 'death' are functionally interchangeable in the Syndicate's legal framework — those who cannot pay their debts become thrulls, and death does not dissolve the contract.

Thrulls in Orzhov lore are constructed from the remnants of past debtors — flesh melted down, recombined, and reborn as mindless servants. They occupy the absolute bottom rung of the Orzhov hierarchy. The Kingpin's Pet's gold chain collar and spiked foot-rings are canonical Orzhov thrall-aesthetic markers: visual indicators of ownership embedded in the creature's body. Skilled death-priests (thrull-fashioners) create flying variants like this one for use as mounts and couriers. [MTG Wiki: Orzhov Syndicate, via WebSearch; Scryfall flavor text]
sources: MTG Wiki: Orzhov Syndicate, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: Extort — the Orzhov mechanic in Gatecrash — reads: whenever you cast a spell, you may pay {W/B}; if you do, each opponent loses 1 life and you gain that much life. The mechanic mechanically literalizes the guild's extractive theology: every action the Orzhov take bleeds opponents and enriches the caster. EDHREC rank 17328 [Scryfall]; Pauper-legal and Paupercommander-legal. A functional extort enabler in budget Orzhov decks.
